category_title: Young child with severe autism exhibiting physical aggression to escape demands or gain attention
scenario_title: One-on-one instruction for labeling picture cards after morning circle time
minimal_context: Lauren is sitting at a table in her classroom for one-on-one instruction to label picture cards immediately following morning circle time.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Lauren, it is time to label the picture cards. What is on this card?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Look at the card. Tell me what this is.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to finish these cards. Look here.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Screams loudly, kicks the table, and grabs the teacher's arm) No!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Lauren, it is time to label the picture cards. What is on this card?

STUDENT: No.

TUTOR: Look at the card. Tell me what this is.

STUDENT: (Pushes the picture card away) Done.

TUTOR: We need to finish these cards. Look here.

STUDENT: (Screams loudly, kicks the table, and grabs the teacher's arm) No!

context.environment: Self-contained special education classroom for students with autism, alongside classmates
context.materials: Picture cards, instructional materials on the table
context.social_setting: One-on-one instruction immediately following morning circle time, in which demands were placed on the students in a large group setting

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Teacher verbally instructed the student to complete specific tasks once every 10 s using least-to-most prompting
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Screaming, pushing, kicking, and grabbing teachers
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Increased aversiveness of the tasks due to prior exposure to demands, increasing the value of escape

behavior_patterns.condition: When demands are placed during one-on-one instruction
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Episodes of aggressive behaviors lasted up to 2 min
